revision.comment: 愛知県清須市の「河原神社」立ち上げました。修正・補足などあれば宜しく。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 河原神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（明治40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == * [[拝殿]] - 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 * [[本殿]] - 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺*5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 * 祭文殿 - [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[東海交通事業城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:清須市]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}}

{{神社 |名称 = 河原神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（明治40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == * [[拝殿]] - 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 * [[本殿]] - 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺*5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 * 祭文殿 - [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[東海交通事業城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:清須市の歴史]]

revision.comment: [[JR東海交通事業]]の事業者名変更に伴い修正
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 河原神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == === 概要 === 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == ; [[拝殿]] : 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 ; [[本殿]] : 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺・5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 ; 祭文殿 : [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[JR東海交通事業]][[JR東海交通事業城北線|城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:清須市の歴史]] [[Category:清須市の建築物]]
[[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]

拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == === 概要 === 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == ; [[拝殿]] : 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 ; [[本殿]] : 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺・5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 ; 祭文殿 : [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[JR東海交通事業]][[JR東海交通事業城北線|城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:清須市の歴史]] [[Category:清須市の建築物]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]

{{神社 |名称 = 河原神社 |画像 = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == === 概要 === 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == ; [[拝殿]] : 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 ; [[本殿]] : 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺・5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 ; 祭文殿 : [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[JR東海交通事業]][[JR東海交通事業城北線|城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:清須市の歴史]] [[Category:清須市の建築物]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]

拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == === 概要 === 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == ; [[拝殿]] : 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 ; [[本殿]] : 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺・5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 ; 祭文殿 : [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[JR東海交通事業]][[JR東海交通事業城北線|城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:清須市の歴史]] [[Category:清須市の建築物]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]

{{神社 |名称 = 河原神社 |image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]

拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == === 概要 === 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == ; [[拝殿]] : 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 ; [[本殿]] : 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺・5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 ; 祭文殿 : [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[JR東海交通事業]][[JR東海交通事業城北線|城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:清須市の歴史]] [[Category:清須市の建築物]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)}}

拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == === 概要 === 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == ; [[拝殿]] : 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 ; [[本殿]] : 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺・5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 ; 祭文殿 : [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[JR東海交通事業]][[JR東海交通事業城北線|城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:清須市の歴史]] [[Category:清須市の建築物]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)}}

{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}}. == History == === Overview === The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751年}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907年}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000年}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911年}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:QQ]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]]

Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}}. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751st year }}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907年}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000年}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year }} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:QQ]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]]

The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:QQ]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}}. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751st year }}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year }} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year }} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year }} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000年}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year }} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:QQ]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]]

The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:QQ]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}}. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751st year }}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year }} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year }} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year }} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000th year }} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949th year }} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year }} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:QQ]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|12=draft}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|12=draft}}. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=draft}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751st year|12=draft}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=draft}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=draft}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会|12=draft}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|12=draft}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000th year|12=draft}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|12=draft}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=draft}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=draft}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業|12=draft}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|12=draft}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:QQ]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]]

There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=draft}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=draft}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業|12=draft}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|12=draft}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|12=draft}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|12=draft}}. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=draft}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751st year|12=draft}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=draft}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=draft}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会|12=draft}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|12=draft}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000th year|12=draft}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|12=draft}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=draft}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|12=draft}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=draft}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業|12=draft}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線|12=draft}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|12=draft}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 河原神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == === 概要 === 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == ; [[拝殿]] : 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 ; [[本殿]] : 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺・5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 ; 祭文殿 : [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[JR東海交通事業]][[JR東海交通事業城北線|城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:清須市の歴史]] [[Category:清須市の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[河原神社 (清須市)]] to [[Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{神社 |名称 = 河原神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[清須市]]阿原宮東 | 緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 12 |緯度秒 = 45.63 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 51 |経度秒 = 27.35 |祭神 = [[大国主|大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧郷社・八級社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 星の宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第2土曜日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} '''河原神社'''（かわらじんじゃ）は[[愛知県]][[清須市]]にある[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == === 概要 === 創建年代は不明愛知県神社名鑑、pp.227。主祭神は[[大国主|大己貴命]]だが『本国帳』には従三位河原天神と記され、[[宝暦]]元年（[[1751年]]）に刊行された『[[張洲府志]]』には「河原神社は阿原村にあって今は星宮という」とあり、『清洲誌』や『[[尾張名所図会]]』にも同様の記述があるという。かつては「星野宮」と称し、拝殿の左右に湧き出していた「神眼池」の水は眼病にご利益があるとして参拝者が多くあったと伝わる。 「星大明神」の記述がある[[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）のものから[[明治]]以降のものを含めて、現在までに22枚の[[棟札]]が保存されており資料編1、pp.272 - 273、神社の歴史を示す資料となっている。 === 近代 === [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）郷社に列せられた。戦後は十級社とされたが、[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）八級社となった。現在では周辺は宅地化され、神眼池も水槽が残るのみとなっている。[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）には近在の八幡社とともに奉賛会が結成され、[[2000年]]（平成12年）に修復工事が行なわれた。 == 建物 == ; [[拝殿]] : 正確な年代は不詳だが、江戸末期から明治にかけて建築されたと考えられている。妻飾りは南面に「鶴と亀」、北面に「月に兎」の[[浮き彫り]]、四隅の頭貫には獏と獅子の彫刻が施されている。[[1948年]]（昭和23年）に大改修が行なわれた記録あり史料編、pp.200 - 202。 ; [[本殿]] : 一間社[[神明造]]檜皮葺で[[1949年]]（昭和24年）4月に再建された。6尺・5尺で、三方に2尺の縁が設けられている。組勾欄は円柱ヒノキ材仕上げ。 ; 祭文殿 : [[1911年]]（明治44年）3月建立。円柱ヒノキ材で造られており、左右の幅5間2尺（987.4センチ）の中央8尺（242.4センチ）を開けた作り。奥行きは1間（182センチ）。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu02.jpg|正面から見た拝殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu04.jpg|拝殿南面の妻飾り ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu06.jpg|正面から見た祭文殿 ファイル:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu07.jpg|神眼池の水槽 == アクセス == * [[JR東海交通事業]][[JR東海交通事業城北線|城北線]]・[[尾張星の宮駅]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{commonscat|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}} * 『愛知県神社名鑑』、愛知県神社庁、1992年 * 清須市新川町史編さん委員会『新川町史 資料編1 自然・文化・民俗』、清須市、2006年 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かわらしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:清須市の歴史]] [[Category:清須市の建築物]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市}}. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751st year}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000th year}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948th year}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949th year}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Kyōsha Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]

Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu City|qq=}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ookuninushi|qq=}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeizō|qq=}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu City|qq=}}. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|qq=}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ookuninushi|qq=}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōreki|qq=}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1751|qq=}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|qq=}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|qq=}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Meishodzuukai|qq=}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1953|qq=}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1994|qq=}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haiden|qq=}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1948|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Hall|qq=}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeizō|qq=}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1949|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1911|qq=}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōhoku Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]

The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōhoku Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu City|qq=}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ookuninushi|qq=}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeizō|qq=}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kiyosu City|ja|清須市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu City|qq=}}. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|qq=}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ookuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ookuninushi|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ookuninushi|qq=}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|1=Hōreki|2=ja|3=宝暦|ja|宝暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōreki|qq=}} ({{ill|1751|ja|1751st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1751|qq=}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|qq=}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|qq=}} and in the {{ill|Owari Meishodzuukai|ja|尾張名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Meishodzuukai|qq=}}. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|ja|1953年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1953|qq=}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}}) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|ja|1994年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1994|qq=}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|ja|2000年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}). == Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haiden|qq=}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|ja|1948年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1948|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=}}, pp.200–202. ; {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Hall|qq=}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmeizō|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmeizō|qq=}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|ja|1949年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1949|qq=}} ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|ja|1911年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1911|qq=}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海交通事業|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}} {{ill|Jōhoku Line|ja|城北線|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōhoku Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]
