{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]] Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]] | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]]. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|qq=}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|qq=}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|qq=}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). == Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=}}, pp.200–202. ; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]] Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]] | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]]. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|qq=}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|qq=}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|qq=}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). == Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=}}, pp.200–202. ; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]]

In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). == Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=}}, pp.200–202. ; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kiyosu]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu]] {{Commons category|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}}

{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]] Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]] | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]]. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). == Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202. ; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kiyosu City]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kiyosu]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu]] {{Commons category|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}}

There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202. ; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:To Be Resorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kiyosu]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu]] {{Commons category|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category To Be Resorted
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = [[File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu03.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]] Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]] | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi }} '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]]. == History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya". Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers. === Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). == Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202. ; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kiyosu]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu]] {{Commons category|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}}

The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23){{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202. ; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood. ; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm). == Gallery == File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike == Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} == References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006 {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|河原神社 (清須市)|version=102072306|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kawahara Shrine (Kiyosu)]] [[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kiyosu]] [[Category:History of Kiyosu]] {{Commons category|Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)]][[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209213]] Wikidata name: Kawara jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17209213]] Wikidata name: Kawara jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu03.jpg | Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]] Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]] | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi | prefecture= {{ill|Kiyosu|qid=Q502407}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.212675|N|136.857597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity= {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944}} | architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]].
== History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.
=== Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]).
== Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike </gallery>
== Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Owari-Hoshinomiya Station|WD=Q7114283}}
== References == {{Citation Help}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{commonscat|Kawara Shrine (Kiyosu)}} * 'Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory', Aichi Prefecture Shrine Office, 1992 * 'Kiyosu Shinkawamachi Historical Materials Compilation 1: Nature, Culture, and Folklore', compiled by the Kiyosu City Shinkawa Town History Committee, Kiyosu City, 2006

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)]][[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209213]] Wikidata name: Kawara jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209213]] Wikidata name: Kawara jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu03.jpg | Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]] Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]] | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi | prefecture= {{ill|Kiyosu|WD=Q502407}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.212675|N|136.857597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity= {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]].
== History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.
=== Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]).
== Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike </gallery>

In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]).
== Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. '''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]].
== History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.
=== Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]).
== Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)]][[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209213]] Wikidata name: Kawara jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209213]] Wikidata name: Kawara jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu03.jpg | Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]] Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]] | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi | prefecture= {{ill|Kiyosu|WD=Q502407}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.212675|N|136.857597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity= {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]].
== History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.
=== Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]).
== Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike </gallery>
== Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] [[:en:Jōhoku Line|Jōhoku Line]] and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Owari-Hoshinomiya Station|WD=Q7114283}}

After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]).
== Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.

'''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]].
== History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.
=== Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]).
== Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)]][[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)]][[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kawara jinja (Kiyosu)]][[ja:河原神社 (清須市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209213]] Wikidata name: Kawara jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17209213]] Wikidata name: Kawara jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu03.jpg | Location = [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]][[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]] Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]] | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi | prefecture= {{ill|Kiyosu|WD=Q502407}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.212675|N|136.857597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity= {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]].
== History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.
=== Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]).
== Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike </gallery>

'''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]] [[:en:Kiyosu|Kiyosu City]].
== History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ookuninushi]], although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] ([[:en:1751|1751]]), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meishodzuukai]]<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.
=== Modern Period === In [[:en:1907|1907]] (the 40th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in [[:en:1953|1953]] (the 28th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]]) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In [[:en:1994|1994]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in [[:en:2000|2000]] (the 12th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]).
== Buildings == ; [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in [[:en:1948|1948]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; [[:en:Honden|Main Hall]] : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmeizō]] style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April [[:en:1949|1949]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March [[:en:1911|1911]] (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike </gallery>

'''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Kiyosu|en|Kiyosu|WD=Q502407|lt=Kiyosu City}}.

== History ==
== Overview ==
The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} ({{ill|1751|en|1751|WD=Q7615|lt=1751}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the {{ill|Owari meisho zue|en|Owari meisho zue|WD=Q11465290|lt=Owari Meishodzuukai}}<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.

=== Modern Period ===
In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}}) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}).

== Buildings ==
; {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden}}
: The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|en|1948|WD=Q5165|lt=1948}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.

: The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmeizō}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|en|1949|WD=Q5188|lt=1949}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.

; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}}
: Built in March {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Kawahara Shrine | image = File:Kawara-Jinja Kiyosu03.jpg | Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|Kiyosu|en|Kiyosu|WD=Q502407|lt=Kiyosu City}} Aharamiya Higashi | Latitude = 35 | Minutes = 12 | Seconds = 45.63 | Longitude = 136 | Minutes = 51 | Seconds = 27.35 | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}} | Shintai = | Shrine Rank = Former Kyōsha, Hachi-kyūsha | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmeizō}} | Alternate Name = Star Shrine | Fuda etc = | Annual Festival = Second Saturday of October | Rituals = | Map = Japan, Aichi | prefecture= {{ill|Kiyosu|WD=Q502407}} | coordinates= {{Coord|35.212675|N|136.857597|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity= {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Kiyosu|en|Kiyosu|WD=Q502407|lt=Kiyosu City}}.
== History == == Overview == The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} ({{ill|1751|en|1751|WD=Q7615|lt=1751}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the {{ill|Owari meisho zue|en|Owari meisho zue|WD=Q11465290|lt=Owari Meishodzuukai}}<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.
=== Modern Period === In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}}) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}).
== Buildings == ; {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden}} : The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|en|1948|WD=Q5165|lt=1948}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.
; {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Main Hall}} : The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmeizō}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|en|1949|WD=Q5188|lt=1949}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.
; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}} : Built in March {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu02.jpg|Haiden as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu04.jpg|South side decorative ornamentation of the Haiden File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu06.jpg|Ritual Hall as seen from the front File:Kawara-Jinja_Kiyosu07.jpg|Water tank of Shingan Ike </gallery>
== Access == * [[:en:JR-Central Transport Service Company|JR Central]] {{ill|Jōhoku Line|en|Jōhoku Line|WD=Q6268862|lt=Jōhoku Line}} and {{ill|Owari Hoshinomiya Station|ja|尾張星の宮駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Hoshinomiya Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Owari-Hoshinomiya Station|WD=Q7114283}}

'''Kawahara Shrine''' (河原神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Kiyosu|en|Kiyosu|WD=Q502407|lt=Kiyosu City}}.

== History ==
== Overview ==
The founding date is unknown<ref name="meikan">{{ill|Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja|愛知県神社名鑑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.227</ref>. The main enshrined deity is {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ookuninushi}}, although the ''Honkokuchō'' records it as Juusan-i Kawahara Tenjin<ref name="meikan"/>. In the first year of {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} ({{ill|1751|en|1751|WD=Q7615|lt=1751}}), the published {{ill|Chōshūfushi|ja|張洲府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshūfushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states, "Kawahara Shrine is located in Aharamura and is now known as Hoshimiya"<ref name="meikan"/>. Similar descriptions are also found in the {{ill|Kiyosu Chronicles|ja|清洲誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosu Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and in the {{ill|Owari meisho zue|en|Owari meisho zue|WD=Q11465290|lt=Owari Meishodzuukai}}<ref name="meikan"/>. It was formerly known as "Hoshinomiya," and the water from the "Shingan Ike" that once gushed from both sides of the haiden was believed to bring relief for eye diseases, attracting many worshippers<ref name="meikan"/>.

=== Modern Period ===
In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a kyōsha<ref name="meikan"/>. After World War II, it was classified as a jūkkyūsha, but in {{ill|1953|en|1953|WD=Q18585|lt=1953}} (the 28th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}}) it became an hachikyūsha<ref name="meikan"/>. Today, the surrounding area has been urbanized, and only the water tank of Shingan Ike remains. In {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}), a support association was formed together with the nearby {{ill|Yahata Shrine|ja|八幡社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahata Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮|1=Hachiman shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390939}}, and restoration work was carried out in {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}} (the 12th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}).

== Buildings ==
; {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Haiden}}
: The exact date is unknown, but it is believed to have been constructed from the late Edo period to the Meiji period. The decorative ornamentation features "crane and turtle" on the south side and "rabbit on the moon" on the north side, while the beam caps at the four corners are adorned with carvings of a baku and a lion. There is a record of a major renovation carried out in {{ill|1948|en|1948|WD=Q5165|lt=1948}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 23)<ref name="shiryo">{{ill|Historical Materials Compilation|ja|史料編|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Materials Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, pp.200–202</ref>.

; {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Main Hall}}
: The main hall, a one-bay shrine in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmeizō}} style with cypress bark roofing, was rebuilt in April {{ill|1949|en|1949|WD=Q5188|lt=1949}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 24). It measures 6 shaku by 5 shaku, with a 2-shaku eave on three sides. The supporting handrail is finished with round cypress wood<ref name="shiryo"/>.

; {{ill|Saimon-den|ja|祭文殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saimon-den|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭文|1=saimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17991683}}
: Built in March {{ill|1911|en|1911|WD=Q2076|lt=1911}} (Meiji 44), it is constructed with round cypress wood, featuring a design with a central opening of 8 shaku flanked by sections measuring 5 ken 2 shaku (987.4 cm) on each side. The depth is 1 ken (182 cm)<ref name="shiryo"/>.
